title: Gatekeeper rejects v1.3.0 release zip due to AppleDouble files in Sparkle.framework
author: SSakutaro
body:
Hi, I was checking Clarc for a possible Homebrew Cask submission and found that the v1.3.0 release zip is rejected by Gatekeeper.

Homebrew Cask requires apps to pass Gatekeeper checks on supported macOS versions/platforms, so this currently blocks a Homebrew Cask submission.

Environment:
- Clarc v1.3.0
- Downloaded from the GitHub release zip
- macOS Sequoia or later

Gatekeeper check:

```sh
spctl -a -vvv -t execute ~/Downloads/Clarc.app
```
Output:

```
/Users/home/Downloads/Clarc.app: rejected (unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework)
origin=Developer ID Application: TNEAR inc. (27WJDMWS6V)
```

codesign --verify reports the same root cause:

```
codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=2 ~/Downloads/Clarc.app
```

Output:

```
/Users/home/Downloads/Clarc.app: unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework In subcomponent: /Users/home/Downloads/Clarc.app/Contents/Frameworks/Sparkle.framework
```

The release zip contains AppleDouble metadata files directly inside Sparkle.framework, for example:

Clarc.app/Contents/Frameworks/Sparkle.framework/._Autoupdate
Clarc.app/Contents/Frameworks/Sparkle.framework/._Updater.app
Clarc.app/Contents/Frameworks/Sparkle.framework/._XPCServices
Clarc.app/Contents/Frameworks/Sparkle.framework/._Resources
Clarc.app/Contents/Frameworks/Sparkle.framework/._Sparkle
Clarc.app/Contents/Frameworks/Sparkle.framework/Versions/._Current

I tested a copy of the app with only these ._* files removed. After removal, the same checks passed:

```
codesign: valid on disk; satisfies its Designated Requirement
spctl: accepted, source=Notarized Developer ID
stapler: The validate action worked!
```

Could you recreate the release zip without AppleDouble/resource-fork metadata files?

For example, the release packaging step could remove ._* files before archiving, or create the zip with macOS metadata disabled, such as:

```
find Clarc.app -name '._*' -delete
COPYFILE_DISABLE=1 zip -r Clarc-1.3.0.zip Clarc.app
```

or with ditto options that avoid preserving resource forks / extended attributes.

After recreating the zip, these checks should pass on the final downloaded app:

```
codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=2 Clarc.app
spctl -a -vvv -t execute Clarc.app
stapler validate Clarc.app
```

This should make the release suitable for Homebrew Cask submission.

Refs:
- Homebrew Acceptable Casks: https://docs.brew.sh/Acceptable-Casks
- Homebrew deprecate/disable reasons: https://docs.brew.sh/Deprecating-Disabling-and-Removing

title: This is more of a feature request
author: trivedip
body: There seems to be no way to see thinking trace, is this something on the horizon ? or is this something I can already do and am struggling to enable ?

title: Bug: `env: node: No such file or directory` when starting chat
author: nerdhaze
body:
Full transparency, I used Claude to help me figure out the issue, and generate this bug report. Love the app!

### Description

Starting a chat in the main chat window always fails immediately with:

```
env: node: No such file or directory
```

The built-in terminal sidebar works fine.

### Root cause

macOS GUI apps launch with a minimal system PATH (`/usr/bin`, `/bin`, `/usr/sbin`, `/sbin`, `/usr/local/bin`) and do not inherit the user's shell PATH from `.zshrc` or `.bash_profile`. When `ClaudeService` spawns the Claude Code CLI subprocess, it cannot find `node` or `claude` if they are installed outside those standard locations.

This affects most users since common install paths are not in the GUI app PATH:
- `claude` via npm: typically `~/.npm-global/bin` or `~/.local/bin`
- `node` via Homebrew: `/opt/homebrew/bin`
- `node` via nvm: `~/.nvm/versions/node/.../bin`

### Workaround

Create symlinks to the affected binaries in `/usr/local/bin`:

```bash
sudo ln -s $(which node) /usr/local/bin/node
sudo ln -s $(which claude) /usr/local/bin/claude
```

### Suggested fix

Have `ClaudeService` resolve the user's full shell PATH before spawning the subprocess, e.g.:

```swift
let shellPath = try await resolveShellPath() // run `zsh -l -c 'echo $PATH'`
var env = ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment
env["PATH"] = shellPath
process.environment = env
```

This is a standard pattern for macOS GUI apps that need to spawn CLI tools installed by the user's package manager.

title: After resuming, only new conversations are created when continuing the chat.
author: xonic789
body: Thanks Clarc! As with what I used in Claude Code, after pressing the + button, I run the /resume command in the directory I was working in with Claude Code. After an error occurs, if I continue the chat, it keeps restarting.

title: Do not create an attachment when pasting an URL
author: AlexanderWillner
body:
**Version:** Version 1.1.9 (15)

**Steps that will reproduce the problem:**

1. Create New Chat
2. Paste https://github.com/ttnear/Clarc/issues/new


**What is the expected result:**

Text "https://github.com/ttnear/Clarc/issues/new" in the chat input field


**What happens instead:**

Shows as attachment


**Possible workaround:**

Paste "https://github.com/ttnear/Clarc/issues/new foobar"


**Any additional information:**


There are other use cases where attachments are generated. An option to disable this behavior would be nice.

title: Feature request: adjust font size
author: fwwhm8f98f-ux
body:
I have been using Clarc for a bit and I really like it. Lot's of useful and handy extra's together in one shell. Well done, thnx.

One thing that would help me if it would be possible to adjust the font size of the dialogue window.

title: URLs do not open in browser
author: erikng
body: Fantastic tool, I've been playing with it all day. I noticed when working with Claude that if it generates something like a tool that has a url, when I click on that link, it opens a blank tiny modal window. I think instead of trying to render it in-line, which will also require Webkit APIs, it should just open in a browser like claude code does.
